metadata.description: Marketplace of Claude Code / Cowork plugins for preparing U.S. court documents, organized one plugin per state, plus two shared federal-law reference plugins: claude-legal-federal-laws (canonical federal debt/consumer-finance corpora plus a nationwide consumer credit-report-rights skills layer under the FCRA) and claude-legal-immigration-laws (canonical immigration rules — INA / 8 CFR / 22 CFR / FAM — plus an on-demand case-law index for the federal circuits, the Board of Immigration Appeals, and the USCIS AAO). Eight state plugins today: wa-court-docs (Washington — GR 14-compliant pleadings, KCDC + KCSC + the populous-counties roll-up, RCW 19.16 / WA CPA consumer-debt defense bundle), or-court-docs (Oregon — UTCR 2.010-compliant pleadings, Multnomah + Washington County Circuit Court specifics + the populous-counties roll-up, ORS 697 / UTPA consumer-debt defense bundle), ca-court-docs (California — CRC 2.100-2.119-compliant pleadings, LASC + SFSC + populous-counties roll-up, Rosenthal Act / FDBPA / CDCLA / UCL consumer-debt defense bundle), co-court-docs (Colorado — C.R.C.P. 10 + CJD 11-01-compliant pleadings, Denver + Arapahoe + populous-counties roll-up, CFDCPA / CCPA / UCCC consumer-debt defense bundle, plus a UDMA-based family-law bundle), in-court-docs (Indiana — Ind. Trial R. 5(E) pleadings under Indiana Trial Rules with the Jarboe burden-shifting and T.R. 59 motion-to-correct-error quirks, Marion Superior Court / Lake Superior Court + populous-counties roll-up, IUCCC / DCSA consumer-debt defense bundle leaning on chain-of-title + FDCPA since Indiana has no collection-agency licensing regime), and ny-court-docs (New York — 22 NYCRR § 202.5 paper format + 22 NYCRR § 202.5-b NYSCEF electronic-filing format with the New York caption and "-against-" party separator, five flagship Supreme Court venues each as its own skill — New York County (1st JD, Manhattan, Commercial Division at the $500k threshold), Kings County (2nd JD, Brooklyn, with CPLR 3408 foreclosure conferences), Bronx County (12th JD), Nassau County (10th JD, Mineola, Commercial Division at $200k), and Queens County (11th JD, Jamaica) — plus dedicated venue skills for the rest of NY's fragmented civil-court system — ny-nyc-civil-court (Civil Court of the City of New York; Civil Court Act $50k cap, 5 boroughs, the highest-volume consumer-debt forum in the country), ny-nyc-housing-court (Housing Part; RPAPL Article 7 summary proceedings; Local Law 136 universal Right to Counsel; ERAP stays), ny-nassau-dc + ny-suffolk-dc (Long Island District Courts under UDCA / 22 NYCRR Part 212), ny-city-courts (~60 upstate City Courts under UCCA / 22 NYCRR Part 210, $15k cap), ny-justice-courts (~1,250 Town and Village Justice Courts under UJCA / 22 NYCRR Part 214, $3k cap), and ny-family-court (FCA Articles 3-10; CSSA; family-offense Orders of Protection; abuse and neglect) — plus a long-tail Supreme Court roll-up (ny-county-courts) for Suffolk / Westchester / Erie / Monroe / Onondaga Supreme Court Civil Term; **five subject-matter bundles**: ny-consumer-debt (FDCPA / Reg F / 2022 CCFA / GBL § 600 / GBL § 349 / CPLR 4544 / UCC Article 9 chain of title); ny-landlord-tenant (RPAPL Article 7 / 2019 HSTPA / 2024 Good Cause Eviction / RPL § 235-b / Local Law 136 / ERAP); ny-personal-injury (CPLR Article 14-A pure comparative fault + Article 16, Insurance Law § 5102(d) no-fault threshold, Labor Law § 240(1) scaffold law, GML § 50-e Notice of Claim, CPLR 214-a medical malpractice, CVA / ASA revival windows); ny-employment (NYS HRL, NYC HRL with *Williams* construed-broadly rule, Labor Law § 191/198/740 + WARN, CROWN Act); ny-commercial-disputes (22 NYCRR § 202.70 Commercial Division + Appendix A; CPLR 3016(b) fraud particularity; BCL § 720 / § 1104-a derivative + dissolution; Faithless Servant; GOL §§ 5-1401 / 5-1402 destination-forum)). All seven state plugins depend on the shared claude-legal-federal-laws plugin for FDCPA / FCRA / TILA / ECOA / EFTA / Garnishment + Reg B-F-V-Z-DD-E-M-N-P-X + RESPA + SCRA + FHA + FTC Telemarketing Sales Rule + Title 11 Bankruptcy + model UCC content — declared in their plugin.json and dereferenced from in-tree symlinks at install time, so the federal corpora live in one canonical place. All state plugins are architected as matter-neutral civil-procedure skills plus subject-matter bundles. The structure leaves clean slots for plugins covering additional states. The seventh plugin, oh-court-docs (Ohio — Ohio Civ. R. 10 + per-court local rules, 8 flagship Court of Common Pleas venues + county-courts roll-up + oh-municipal-courts + oh-family-court, R.C. Chapter 1345 CSPA consumer-debt defense bundle + R.C. Title 31 + Title 33 family-law bundle). The eighth plugin, tn-court-docs (Tennessee — no statewide format rule; Tenn. R. Civ. P. 10/11 + local rules; Circuit / Chancery / General Sessions trial-court layers with the $25,000 General Sessions cap and 10-day de novo appeal; dedicated venue skills for Davidson / Shelby / Knox / Hamilton + a General Sessions skill + split family-court / juvenile-court skills; **six subject-matter bundles** — tn-consumer-debt (TCPA + Tennessee Collection Service Act + the 2024 § 20-6-104 debt-buyer rule + chain of title), tn-family-law (Title 36 equitable distribution + income-shares child support + four alimony types + parenting plans), tn-landlord-tenant (URLTA at § 66-28 + detainer warrants + CARES Act 30-day notice overlay + self-help eviction § 66-28-504), tn-personal-injury (McIntyre 49%-bar comparative fault + GTLA + Health Care Liability Act + 2011 tort-reform caps upheld in McClay), tn-employment (THRA + TPPA whistleblower + Wage Regulation Act + Udom non-compete + Workers' Comp + the post-7/1/14 Court of Workers' Compensation Claims forum), and tn-commercial-disputes (Chancery + Davidson Business Court + Pursell B2B TCPA exclusion + TUTSA trade secrets + UVTA fraudulent transfer + Tennessee Revised LLC Act + Tennessee BCA + Rule 9.02 fraud particularity + Tennessee Uniform Arbitration Act)).

plugins.name: claude-legal-federal-laws
plugins.description: Shared reference corpus for federal U.S. debt-collection and consumer-finance law (FDCPA, FCRA, TILA, ECOA, Reg B/F/V/Z, plus RESPA, FHA, SCRA, FTC Telemarketing Sales Rule), the federal Bankruptcy Code (Title 11), and the model Uniform Commercial Code (Articles 1, 2, 3, 9). State plugins declare this as a dependency; the marketplace runtime auto-installs it and dereferences the symlinks each state plugin uses to point into this directory. Also ships a nationwide, venue-independent **consumer credit-report-rights skills layer** built on the FCRA (15 U.S.C. §§ 1681 et seq.): consumer-report-ordering (order all Big-3 + specialty CRA reports and the free-report entitlements), consumer-credit-disputes (lawful direct-to-CRA disputes under § 1681i, the 4-business-day identity-theft block under § 1681c-2, and post-dispute regulator escalation), consumer-report-accuracy (PII hygiene, the Date of First Delinquency clock + re-aging, and the 'disputed by consumer' flag), consumer-harm-documentation (communication logs, damages ledgers, and harm declarations), and consumer-credit-monitoring (generating adverse-action proof, § 1681i(d) re-notification, and ongoing review). These skills produce documents, not advice, and ride along anywhere a dependent state plugin is installed.

plugins.name: claude-legal-immigration-laws
plugins.description: Shared reference corpus for U.S. immigration law — the federal, venue-independent counterpart to claude-legal-federal-laws, built for immigration practice. Snapshots the canonical **rules** verbatim as Markdown: the Immigration and Nationality Act (INA, 8 U.S.C. Chapter 12, one file per subchapter with an INA-section ↔ 8 U.S.C.-section crosswalk), the immigration **regulations** in 8 CFR (DHS/USCIS/CBP/ICE chapter I plus the DOJ/EOIR immigration-court and Board of Immigration Appeals chapter V at 8 CFR 1001-1337) and the State Department visa/passport/exchange parts of 22 CFR, and the **Foreign Affairs Manual (FAM)** verbatim (9 FAM 100/302/402/502/504 + 8 FAM 300 + 7 FAM 000/1400; fam.state.gov omits the Sectigo intermediate from its TLS chain, so scripts/pull_fam.py AIA-chases it — reads the leaf's AIA URL, fetches the intermediate, adds it to a verify bundle — then crawls the site's JSON table-of-contents API). Indexes the immigration **case-law** sources for on-demand lookup rather than snapshotting them: the federal circuit courts (petitions for review of removal orders under INA § 242 / 8 U.S.C. § 1252, via CourtListener and the connected CourtListener MCP), the Board of Immigration Appeals precedent decisions (I&N Dec., via the EOIR Virtual Law Library), and the USCIS Administrative Appeals Office (AAO) decisions. It also carries a court-rules corpus for the EOIR immigration courts (the binding 8 CFR Part 1003/1240/1208 regulations plus pointer stubs for the Immigration Court Practice Manual and the BIA Practice Manual). As of v0.3.0 the plugin ships an 11-skill, matter-neutral, venue-independent, document-producing self-help layer: immigration-pro-se, eoir-immigration-courts, immigration-deadlines, immigration-fact-check, eoir-removal-defense, eoir-motions-to-reopen-reconsider, bia-appeals, uscis-benefit-requests, immigration-foia, circuit-petition-for-review, and consular-visa-refusal (each produces documents, not advice, and flags when a licensed attorney or EOIR-accredited representative is needed). Pullers are scripts/pull_ina.py, scripts/pull_immigration_cfr.py, scripts/pull_fam.py, and scripts/pull_eoir_manuals.py; case-law access is documented in references/legal-data-apis.md and references/online-sources.md.

plugins.name: wa-court-docs
plugins.description: Draft and format pleadings, declarations, motions, notes for motion docket, and proposed orders for Washington courts across the full civil-practice surface. Applies GR 14 formatting; provides dedicated venue skills for King County District Court (wa-kcdc), King County Superior Court (wa-kcsc), Pierce County Superior Court / Tacoma (wa-pierce), Snohomish County Superior Court / Everett (wa-snohomish), Spokane County Superior Court (wa-spokane), the Superior Court Family Law Department (wa-family-court), and a roll-up for the rest of the state (wa-county-courts). Architected as matter-neutral civil-procedure skills (CR + GR + ER + 35 court-rule sets, fees and costs, local rules, citation format, online sources, discovery, first-30-days response, hearings, filing packets, post-judgment, fact-checking, deadlines, drafting scaffolders) plus six subject-matter bundles: wa-consumer-debt (FDCPA, Reg F, RCW 19.16, Washington CPA, chain of title), wa-family-law (Washington's community-property regime + RCW Title 26 dissolution / parenting / child support framework + the 2022 consolidated RCW 7.105 civil-protection-order regime + RCW 26.26A UPA parentage), wa-landlord-tenant (RCW 59.18 RLTA + the 2019 SB 5600 mandatory-form pay-or-vacate reform + the 2021 SB 5160 just-cause framework + HB 1815 statewide tenant Right to Counsel + ERP), wa-personal-injury (RCW 4.22 pure comparative fault + several-liability post-1986 Reform Act + WPLA at RCW 7.72 + medical malpractice at RCW 7.70 + Notice of Tort Claim under RCW 4.92 / 4.96), wa-employment (Minimum Wage Act + WLAD at RCW 49.60 + PFML under RCW Title 50A + non-compete reform at RCW 49.62 + L&I-exclusive workers' comp at RCW 51), and wa-commercial-disputes (Washington CPA at RCW 19.86 with the Hangman Ridge 5-element test + WBCA at RCW 23B + LLC Act at RCW 25.15 + UCC at RCW 62A + MAR under RCW 7.06). Follows a **thin-skill architecture**: SKILL.md bodies describe procedural frameworks and point at the references corpus for current statutory text, dollar thresholds, day counts, and section subsections rather than embedding them — so quarterly corpus refreshes update the canonical law without requiring skill edits. Reference corpora include 35 court-rule sets covering 1,233+ rules and 92 RCW chapters covering 3,255+ sections, plus the shared federal-debt-laws and ucc-model content via the claude-legal-federal-laws dependency. All workflows are skills (no slash commands) so the agent invokes them automatically from natural-language requests.

plugins.name: or-court-docs
plugins.description: Draft and format pleadings, declarations, motions, notes for hearing, and proposed orders for Oregon circuit courts. Applies UTCR 2.010 statewide formatting; includes Multnomah County Circuit Court (Portland) and Washington County Circuit Court (Hillsboro) specifics plus a county-court roll-up for the other most-populous counties (Clackamas, Lane, Marion, Jackson, Deschutes, Linn, Benton, Yamhill, Polk, Douglas); supports pro se workflows. Architected as matter-neutral civil-procedure skills (ORCP civil rules, OEC evidence rules, ORS 20 fees and costs, local SLR rules, citation format per the Oregon Appellate Courts Style Manual, online sources, discovery, first-30-days response, hearings, filing packets, post-judgment, fact-checking, deadlines under ORCP 10 + ORS 187.010 holidays) plus subject-matter bundles that plug into the procedural skills. The first subject-matter bundle, or-consumer-debt, covers FDCPA, Reg F, ORS 697 (Collection Agency Registration), the Oregon Unlawful Trade Practices Act (UTPA, ORS 646.605 et seq.), chain-of-title doctrine, and fact-pattern triage for debt-buyer defense. The architecture leaves clean slots for future bundles (landlord-tenant under ORS 90, family, personal injury, etc.). All workflows are skills (no slash commands) so the agent invokes them automatically from natural-language requests.

plugins.name: ca-court-docs
plugins.description: Draft and format pleadings, declarations, motions, notices of motion, and proposed orders for California superior courts. Applies California Rules of Court 2.100-2.119 statewide formatting; includes Los Angeles Superior Court (LASC — Stanley Mosk Courthouse) and San Francisco Superior Court (SFSC — Civic Center Courthouse, Dept. 302 law-and-motion) specifics plus a county-court roll-up for the other most-populous counties (Orange, San Diego, Riverside, San Bernardino, Santa Clara, Alameda, Sacramento, Contra Costa, Fresno, Kern); supports pro se ('In Pro Per') workflows. Architected as matter-neutral civil-procedure skills (CCP civil rules with the 35-interrogatory cap and 45-day motion-to-compel-further deadline, CEC evidence rules, ORS 20-style California fee-and-cost framework under CCP §§ 1032/1033.5/§ 1717, local rules for LASC + SFSC + populous counties, citation format per the California Style Manual, CRC 8.1115 unpublished-opinion rule, deadline arithmetic under CCP §§ 12/12a/12c with Govt. Code § 6700 holidays, hearings under the CRC 3.1308 tentative-ruling regime) plus subject-matter bundles that plug into the procedural skills. The first subject-matter bundle, ca-consumer-debt, covers FDCPA, Reg F, the Rosenthal Fair Debt Collection Practices Act (Cal. Civ. Code §§ 1788-1788.33), the Fair Debt Buying Practices Act (Cal. Civ. Code §§ 1788.50-1788.66), the California Debt Collection Licensing Act (Cal. Fin. Code §§ 100000-100027), the Unfair Competition Law (Cal. Bus. & Prof. Code §§ 17200-17210), the Consumers Legal Remedies Act (Cal. Civ. Code §§ 1750-1784), chain-of-title doctrine under Cal. Comm. Code Art. 9, and fact-pattern triage for debt-buyer defense. All workflows are skills (no slash commands) so the agent invokes them automatically from natural-language requests.

plugins.name: co-court-docs
plugins.description: Draft and format pleadings, declarations, motions, notices, and proposed orders for Colorado courts. Applies C.R.C.P. 10 + Chief Justice Directive 11-01 statewide formatting (uniform 1-inch margins, double-spaced 12-point text, the two-block Colorado caption with the case-number / division / courtroom box, Colorado attorney-registration number for licensed counsel under C.R.C.P. 11(a)); includes Denver District Court (2nd Judicial District — Lindsey-Flanigan Courthouse) and Arapahoe County District Court (18th Judicial District — Centennial, Aurora, Littleton) specifics plus a county-courts roll-up for the other most-populous counties (Jefferson, El Paso, Adams, Boulder, Larimer, Douglas, Weld, Pueblo, Mesa, Broomfield); supports pro se / self-represented workflows including CCEFS Pro Se and the Colorado Judicial Branch JDF (Judicial Department Forms) catalog. Architected as matter-neutral civil-procedure skills (C.R.C.P. civil rules with the 21-day answer window under Rule 12(a)(1) and the C.R.C.P. 121 § 1-15 motion-practice timing — 21-day response, 7-day reply, 15-page motion / 10-page reply limits, certificate of conferral under § 1-15(8); CRE evidence rules; fees and costs under C.R.S. art. 16 of title 13; local rules and Chief Justice Directives; Colorado neutral-citation conventions for post-2012 cases at the Colorado Supreme Court and Court of Appeals; deadline arithmetic under C.R.C.P. 6 with C.R.S. § 24-11-101 holidays including Frances Xavier Cabrini Day (replaced Columbus Day in 2020 via H.B. 20-1031) and Juneteenth; hearings under the Cisco Webex statewide remote-appearance protocol with division-by-division practice standards; first-30-days response under C.R.C.P. 12 including the Twombly/Iqbal-equivalent pleading standard adopted in Warne v. Hall, 2016 CO 50; post-judgment under C.R.C.P. 59/60 and the C.R.S. § 13-54.5 garnishment regime with the expanded SB22-086 exemption table including the $250,000 homestead) plus subject-matter bundles. Two subject-matter bundles ship: (1) co-consumer-debt covering FDCPA, Regulation F, the Colorado Fair Debt Collection Practices Act (CFDCPA — C.R.S. art. 16 of title 5, recodified from Title 12 in 2022 by HB22-1024), the Colorado Consumer Protection Act (CCPA — C.R.S. art. 1 of title 6 with treble damages and mandatory fees under C.R.S. § 6-1-113, subject to the Hall v. Walter 'significant public impact' element), the Uniform Consumer Credit Code (UCCC — C.R.S. art. 1-9 of title 5 administered by the Colorado AG), Colorado collection-agency licensure under C.R.S. § 5-16-115 and the AG's Collection Agency Board, chain-of-title doctrine under Colorado UCC Article 9 (C.R.S. art. 9 of title 4) including the bill-of-sale-and-assignment foundation, the 6-year SOL on liquidated debt under C.R.S. § 13-80-103.5(1)(a) as settled in Hassler v. Account Brokers of Larimer Cnty., the 5-pattern fact-pattern triage (stale credit-card debt, debt buyer with original-creditor contract, medical-debt collection under HB23-1126, default-judgment attack under C.R.C.P. 60(b), FDCPA/CCPA counterclaim package); and (2) co-family-law covering the Uniform Dissolution of Marriage Act (UDMA — C.R.S. art. 10 of title 14) with the 91-day residency requirement under § 14-10-106(1)(a)(I) and the 91-day waiting period under § 14-10-106(1)(a)(III), dissolution of marriage / legal separation under § 14-10-106, declaration of invalidity of marriage (annulment) under § 14-10-111 with the narrow grounds (lack of capacity, impotence, underage, bigamy, prohibited-by-law, jest), C.R.C.P. 16.2 mandatory financial-disclosure regime including the Sworn Financial Statement (JDF 1111) due 42 days after service, allocation of parental responsibilities (replaced 'custody' terminology in 1998) under § 14-10-124 with decision-making and parenting-time allocation, the C.R.S. § 14-10-115 income-shares child-support guideline with the 93-overnight rule and the statewide online worksheet (JDF 1820 E) and imputation of income under § 14-10-115(5)(b), modification of child support requiring a substantial-and-continuing change of >= 10% under § 14-10-122(1)(b), maintenance under the C.R.S. § 14-10-114 guideline framework as amended in 2014 and 2024, common-law marriage under People v. Lucero (1987) as modernized by In re Marriage of Hogsett & Neale, 2021 CO 1, and UCCJEA jurisdiction over children at C.R.S. art. 13 of title 14. All workflows are skills (no slash commands) so the agent invokes them automatically from natural-language requests.

plugins.name: in-court-docs
plugins.description: Draft and format pleadings, declarations, motions, notices, and proposed orders for Indiana courts. Applies Indiana Trial Rule 5(E) formatting (Indiana lacks a single consolidated format rule; the formatting requirements live across T.R. 5(E), T.R. 11, and county local rules); covers Marion Superior Court (Indianapolis, statewide's highest civil-case-volume court — 36 numbered courtrooms in the City-County Building) and Lake Superior Court (Crown Point, with Hammond and East Chicago branches in Indiana's second-largest county), plus a populous-counties roll-up (Allen, Hamilton, St. Joseph, Vanderburgh, Elkhart, Tippecanoe, Porter, Madison) and Bartholomew Circuit Court in the in-county-courts roll-up; supports pro se workflows under the Goossens v. Goossens same-standard rule. Architected as matter-neutral civil-procedure skills (Indiana Trial Rules, Indiana Rules of Evidence, Indiana Code citation conventions, fees and costs under IC 33-37, local rules for Marion and Lake, IC 1-1-9-1 holiday-calendar deadline arithmetic, Odyssey statewide e-filing) plus subject-matter bundles. The in-family-court venue skill covers Indiana's family-law topology: no separate Family Court trial court; large counties (Marion, Lake, Allen, Vanderburgh, Hamilton, St. Joseph) have a dedicated Juvenile Division for JP / JC / JD / JS / JM / JT case types; smaller counties (like Bartholomew) consolidate JP / DC / DR jurisdiction in the Circuit Court. Two subject-matter bundles ship: in-consumer-debt (FDCPA / Reg F / IUCCC at IC 24-4.5 / DCSA at IC 24-5-0.5 / chain of title under Indiana UCC Article 9 at IC 26-1-9.1 / the four debt SOLs at IC 34-11-2; Indiana does NOT require collection-agency licensing — defense focus shifts to chain of title + FDCPA + DCSA), and in-family-law (paternity at IC 31-14 — the JP case-type backbone; dissolution at IC 31-15 under Indiana's equitable-distribution regime; child support at IC 31-16 with the Indiana Child Support Guidelines court rule; custody and parenting time at IC 31-17 with the Indiana Parenting Time Guidelines court rule; adoption at IC 31-19; UCCJEA at IC 31-21; DCS / CHINS / TPR / delinquency at IC 31-25 / 31-30 / 31-32 / 31-34 / 31-35 / 31-37; protection orders at IC 34-26-5). Follows a thin-skill architecture: SKILL.md bodies describe procedural frameworks and point at the references corpus for current statutory text, dollar thresholds, day counts, and section subsections rather than embedding them. All workflows are skills (no slash commands) so the agent invokes them automatically from natural-language requests.

plugins.name: ny-court-docs
plugins.description: Draft and format pleadings, affirmations, affidavits, motions, notices of motion, orders to show cause, and proposed orders for New York courts. Applies 22 NYCRR § 202.5 paper format and 22 NYCRR § 202.5-b NYSCEF electronic-filing format (1-inch margins, double-spaced 12-point text, the New York caption with the "-against-" party separator, the Index Number, the assigned Justice + Part designation); includes five flagship Supreme Court venues each as its own skill — New York County (1st JD, Manhattan, 60 Centre Street, with the $500,000-threshold Commercial Division under 22 NYCRR § 202.70), Kings County (2nd JD, Brooklyn, 360 Adams Street, with the high-volume foreclosure settlement conference part under CPLR 3408), Bronx County (12th JD, 851 Grand Concourse), Nassau County (10th JD, Mineola, with a $200,000-threshold Commercial Division), and Queens County (11th JD, Jamaica) — plus dedicated venue skills for the rest of NY's fragmented civil-court system — ny-nyc-civil-court (Civil Court Act $50k cap, 5 borough branches, the highest-volume consumer-debt forum in the country with 22 NYCRR Part 208 + § 208.6-a default scrutiny), ny-nyc-housing-court (Housing Part of NYC Civil Court — RPAPL Article 7 summary proceedings + Local Law 136 universal Right to Counsel + ERAP stays + rent regulation overlays), ny-nassau-dc and ny-suffolk-dc (Long Island District Courts each as a dedicated skill under UDCA and 22 NYCRR Part 212), ny-city-courts (~60 upstate City Courts under UCCA / 22 NYCRR Part 210, $15k cap), ny-justice-courts (~1,250 Town and Village Justice Courts under UJCA / 22 NYCRR Part 214, $3k cap; the only civil forum for eastern Suffolk County matters under $3k), and ny-family-court (FCA Articles 3-10 / 22 NYCRR Part 205 — CSSA child support, custody, family-offense Orders of Protection, abuse and neglect) — plus a long-tail Supreme Court roll-up (ny-county-courts) covering Suffolk / Westchester / Erie / Monroe / Onondaga / Richmond / Rockland / Albany / Orange / Dutchess / Saratoga / Oneida Supreme Court Civil Term; supports pro se / self-represented workflows including the post-2023 CPLR 2106 universal affirmation under penalty of perjury (L 2023, ch 559 ending the notary-bottleneck for pro se filers). Architected as matter-neutral civil-procedure skills (CPLR civil rules with the broadest "material and necessary" disclosure scope in the U.S. at CPLR 3101(a), the CPLR 3130/3133(b) 25-interrogatory cap, the 22 NYCRR § 202.20-f good-faith-conferral rule, CPLR 3211 pre-answer motion-to-dismiss practice with the strict (e) consolidation rule, the Individual Assignment System under 22 NYCRR § 202.3, Microsoft Teams remote hearings, the Guide to NY Evidence in lieu of a codified evidence code with CPLR Article 45 codified evidence including CPLR 4518 business records and CPLR 4544 small-print contracts; CPLR 2103(b)(2) 5-day mail-service rule longer than the federal/WA/OR 3-day rule; deadline arithmetic under CPLR 2103 with NY Gen. Constr. Law § 24 holidays including the distinctive Lincoln's Birthday (Feb 12) court closure and annual Election Day closure; the Notice of Motion / Order to Show Cause / cross-motion framework under CPLR 2214 and 2215 with the 8-day minimum service period and the 22 NYCRR § 202.8-b 25-page memo / 15-page reply limits; the verified-vs-unverified pleading distinction under CPLR 3020; the CPLR 5015(a)(1)-(5) motion-to-vacate framework with the 1-year clock; CPLR Article 52 enforcement of money judgments including restraining notice (5222), income execution (5231), property execution (5232), the Exempt Income Protection Act (CPLR 5222-a) with the $3,090 automatic-protection threshold and EJ-FOC-1 exemption form, and the 20-year SOL on money judgments at CPLR 211(b); 22 NYCRR § 202.48 settle-order procedure with the jurisdictional 60-day clock) plus subject-matter bundles. Five subject-matter bundles ship: (1) ny-consumer-debt covering FDCPA, Reg F, the **2022 Consumer Credit Fairness Act** (3-year SOL at CPLR 213(a), heightened pleading at CPLR 3015(e), heightened default-judgment evidence at 22 NYCRR § 202.27-a, additional notice mailing at CPLR 308(six)), N.Y. GBL § 600 et seq. collection-agency licensing, N.Y. GBL §§ 349 / 350 deceptive acts and false advertising, CPLR 4544 small-print-contract exclusion, chain of title under N.Y. UCC Article 9 with CPLR 4518 business-records foundation per *Bank of NY Mellon v. Gordon*, 171 AD3d 197 (2d Dept 2019), and 5-pattern fact-pattern triage; (2) ny-landlord-tenant covering RPAPL Article 7 summary proceedings (14-day rent demand under § 711(2), 30/60/90-day holdover notice scaled by tenancy under RPL § 226-c), the **2019 HSTPA** reforms (1-month security-deposit cap at GOL § 7-108(1-a), late-fee cap at RPL § 238-a, bilateral attorney's-fee shifting at RPL § 234, 6-year rent-overcharge lookback), the **2024 Good Cause Eviction Law** at RPL Article 6-A, rent regulation regimes (Rent Stabilization, Rent Control, Loft Law, NYCHA, Section 8, Mitchell-Lama), RPL § 235-b implied warranty of habitability, NYC right-to-counsel under Local Law 136 of 2017 at NYC Admin Code § 26-1301, the Emergency Rental Assistance Program (ERAP) automatic stay, and a comprehensive defenses + counterclaims catalog; (3) ny-personal-injury covering CPLR Article 14-A pure comparative fault + Article 16 several-liability cap on non-economic damages, Insurance Law § 5102(d) no-fault "serious injury" threshold + Article 51 PIP, Labor Law § 240(1) absolute scaffold-law liability + § 241(6) Industrial Code (12 NYCRR Part 23) + § 200 common-law negligence, GML § 50-e 90-day Notice of Claim against state actors, CPLR 214-a 30-month medical-malpractice SOL with continuous-treatment toll, CPLR 214-c discovery-rule toxic-tort SOL, EPTL § 5-4.1 wrongful death (2-year SOL), Child Victims Act (CPLR 214-g) and Adult Survivors Act (CPLR 214-j) revival windows, and the NY-unique Bill of Particulars practice at CPLR §§ 3041-3043; (4) ny-employment covering the NYS Human Rights Law (NY Exec Law § 296, all-size employer post-2019, "petty slights" harassment standard), the NYC Human Rights Law (NYC Admin Code § 8-107 with the *Williams v. NYCHA* construed-broadly rule, mandatory attorney's fees under § 8-502(g), caregiver / salary-history / stalking-victim protections), Labor Law § 191 frequency-of-pay (*Vega* late-payment liquidated damages), § 198 wage theft (6-year SOL, 100/200% liquidated damages), Labor Law § 740 whistleblower (post-2022 expansion + 2-year SOL), NYS WARN Act (50+ employees, 90-day notice), 2018 NYS Sexual Harassment Act, and the CROWN Act (race discrimination by hair); (5) ny-commercial-disputes covering 22 NYCRR § 202.70 Commercial Division (county-by-county thresholds $50k-$500k; Appendix A's 25-page memos, proportionality-in-discovery rule, designated counsel, accelerated adjudication), CPLR 3016(b) fraud particularity, CPLR 213(2) 6-year contract SOL + 213(8) 6/2-year fraud discovery rule, BCL § 720 derivative actions + BCL § 1104-a judicial dissolution with § 1118 buyout election, LLC Law § 702 dissolution, the Faithless Servant Doctrine (*Phansalkar*), GOL §§ 5-1401 / 5-1402 NY-as-destination-forum at $250k / $1M thresholds, and CPLR 5004 9% pre-judgment interest. All workflows are skills (no slash commands) so the agent invokes them automatically from natural-language requests.

plugins.name: oh-court-docs
plugins.description: Draft and format pleadings, declarations, motions, notices, and proposed orders for Ohio courts. Applies Ohio Civ. R. 10 + per-court local rules statewide formatting. Includes eight flagship Court of Common Pleas venues each as its own skill — Cuyahoga (Cleveland), Franklin (Columbus), Hamilton (Cincinnati), Summit (Akron), Montgomery (Dayton), Lucas (Toledo), Stark (Canton), and Butler (Hamilton, OH) — plus a county-courts roll-up for the other 80 Ohio counties, a dedicated `oh-municipal-courts` skill covering the Ohio Municipal Court layer (R.C. Chapter 1901; $15,000 civil cap; $6,000 small-claims division; the primary pro-se consumer-debt forum in most Ohio counties), and an `oh-family-court` skill covering both the Domestic Relations Division of Common Pleas (divorce + married-parent custody/support) and the Juvenile Division (unmarried-parent custody + child-support enforcement + abuse/neglect under R.C. Chapter 2151). Supports pro se workflows. Architected as matter-neutral civil-procedure skills (Ohio Civ. R. with the FRCP-style framework; Ohio Evid. R.; Ohio Rules of Superintendence; CPLR-analog deadline arithmetic per Civ. R. 6; Ohio public-domain citation format `YYYY-Ohio-NNNN` mandatory in appellate cases since 2002) plus two baseline subject-matter bundles. (1) oh-consumer-debt covering FDCPA, Reg F, the **Ohio Consumer Sales Practices Act** (R.C. Chapter 1345) with R.C. 1345.09 treble damages on willful violations and mandatory attorney's fees, chain of title under Ohio UCC Article 9 (R.C. Chapter 1309), and the Ohio statute-of-limitations regime (R.C. 2305.06 six-year written-contract SOL; R.C. 1345.10 two-year CSPA SOL); Ohio has NO collection-agency licensing regime. (2) oh-family-law covering divorce + dissolution + annulment + legal separation under R.C. Chapter 3105 with equitable distribution (R.C. 3105.171; NOT a community-property state); allocation of parental responsibilities under R.C. 3109; child support guideline at R.C. Chapter 3119 (income-shares model with $300,000 combined-income cap and 10% deviation threshold); spousal support under R.C. 3105.18; UCCJEA at R.C. Chapter 3127; UIFSA at R.C. Chapter 3115. All workflows are skills (no slash commands) so the agent invokes them automatically from natural-language requests.

plugins.name: tn-court-docs
plugins.description: Draft and format pleadings, declarations, motions, notices, and proposed orders for Tennessee courts. Tennessee has NO single statewide page/margin/font rule — form of pleadings is governed by Tenn. R. Civ. P. 10 (caption + numbered paragraphs + exhibits) and Rule 11 (signature with the attorney's Board of Professional Responsibility number), with page limits and typography set by each court's LOCAL RULES. Covers Tennessee's three trial-court layers — Circuit Court (law), Chancery Court (equity, with the distinctive Clerk & Master and Chancellor titles), and the limited-jurisdiction General Sessions Court (the dominant consumer-debt and eviction forum: $25,000 civil cap under Tenn. Code Ann. § 16-15-501, unlimited detainer jurisdiction, informal procedure with no formal discovery as of right, suit commenced by a civil warrant, and a 10-day de novo appeal to Circuit Court under § 27-5-108). Ships dedicated venue skills for the four largest counties — Davidson (Nashville, 20th JD, Metro consolidated government, eFileTN/Odyssey Chancery), Shelby (Memphis, 30th JD, eFlex), Knox (Knoxville, 6th JD), and Hamilton (Chattanooga, 11th JD) — plus a `tn-general-sessions` skill, a county-courts roll-up, and split family-forum skills (`tn-family-court` for Circuit/Chancery divorce + `tn-juvenile-court` for Title 37 paternity/dependency). Architected as matter-neutral civil-procedure skills (Tenn. R. Civ. P.; Tenn. R. Evid. with 803(6) business-records + 902 self-authentication; Rule 6.01/6.05 deadline arithmetic with Tenn. Code Ann. § 15-1-101 holidays including Good Friday and Columbus Day; the *Rye v. Women's Care Center of Memphis*, 477 S.W.3d 235 (Tenn. 2015) summary-judgment standard; citation per Tenn. Sup. Ct. R. 4) plus **six subject-matter bundles**. (1) `tn-consumer-debt` — FDCPA, Reg F, the Tennessee Consumer Protection Act (Tenn. Code Ann. § 47-18-101, with the *Pursell v. First American National Bank*, 937 S.W.2d 838 (Tenn. 1996) rule that the TCPA generally does not reach the act of collecting a debt), the Tennessee Collection Service Act licensing regime (Title 62, ch. 20), the 2024 debt-buyer pre-default-judgment documentation requirement (§ 20-6-104), chain of title under Tennessee UCC Article 9, and the 6-year written-contract SOL (§ 28-3-109). (2) `tn-family-law` — equitable distribution (§ 36-4-121; NOT community property), the 60/90-day irreconcilable-differences waiting period (§ 36-4-103), income-shares child support (§ 36-5-101 + Tenn. Comp. R. & Regs. ch. 1240-02-04), the four Tennessee alimony types (§ 36-5-121), permanent parenting plans (§ 36-6-401) with the 2018 relocation framework (§ 36-6-108). (3) `tn-landlord-tenant` — the URLTA (§ 66-28-101, applicable in counties over 75,000 population), non-URLTA counties (§ 66-7-101), the 14-day nonpayment notice (§ 66-28-505), detainer-warrant practice (§ 29-18-101), the CARES Act 30-day notice overlay for federally-subsidized properties confirmed by *Schiminger v. Cooperage Project, LLC* (Tenn. App. 2024), the self-help eviction prohibition under § 66-28-504 with 3-months-or-3x-actual damages, and the § 66-28-514 retaliation presumption. (4) `tn-personal-injury` — *McIntyre v. Balentine*, 833 S.W.2d 52 (Tenn. 1992) modified comparative fault (49% bar), the 1-year tort SOL (§ 28-3-104), the Governmental Tort Liability Act ($300k/$700k caps; § 29-20-101) with bench trial and no jury, the Health Care Liability Act pre-suit notice + certificate of good faith (§ 29-26-121/-122) with the contiguous-state expert rule, the 2011 tort-reform damages caps (§ 29-39-102 non-economic at $750k/$1M catastrophic; § 29-39-104 punitive at greater of $500k or 2x compensatory) upheld in *McClay v. Airport Management Services, LLC*, 596 S.W.3d 686 (Tenn. 2020), the TPLA 10-year statute of repose (§ 29-28-103), and wrongful death under § 20-5-106 with *Jordan v. Baptist Three Rivers Hospital* emotional damages. (5) `tn-employment` — the Tennessee Human Rights Act at § 4-21-101 (8+ employee coverage; 1-year judicial SOL at § 4-21-311; 180-day administrative window to THRC; mandatory attorney's fees), the Tennessee Disability Act at § 8-50-103, the Tennessee Public Protection Act whistleblower statute at § 50-1-304 with the strict 'sole reason' element under *Williams v. City of Burns*, 465 S.W.3d 96 (Tenn. 2015), the Wage Regulation Act at § 50-2-101 (no state minimum wage — FLSA applies; final wages within 21 days of separation; willful-nonpayment liquidated damages), Tennessee's right-to-work statute at § 50-1-201 constitutionalized by the Nov. 2022 amendment to Tenn. Const. art. XI § 21, common-law non-compete enforceability under *Murfreesboro Medical Clinic v. Udom*, 166 S.W.3d 674 (Tenn. 2005), and the workers'-compensation exclusive-remedy regime at § 50-6-101 with the post-7/1/14 Court of Workers' Compensation Claims (CWCC) administrative forum under § 50-6-237. (6) `tn-commercial-disputes` — the Chancery Court as Tennessee's principal commercial-equity forum (with the Davidson Business Court program), the TCPA *Pursell* B2B exclusion, the Tennessee Uniform Trade Secrets Act at § 47-25-1701, the Uniform Voidable Transactions Act (formerly TUFTA) at § 66-3-301, the Tennessee Revised LLC Act at § 48-249-101 and the Tennessee Business Corporation Act at § 48-11-101 (with the 1998 demand-required-no-futility reform for derivative actions), Rule 9.02 fraud-with-particularity pleading, business torts (tortious interference, civil conspiracy, conversion), the Tennessee Uniform Arbitration Act at § 29-5-301 with FAA preemption, and the Tennessee Securities Act at § 48-1-101. All workflows are skills (no slash commands) so the agent invokes them automatically from natural-language requests.
